brief:
I now have the complete picture. Here is the creator brief.

---

# Creator Brief — @elijahaifl (grounds all ideation)

*Source of truth: `team/state-of-play.md` (2026-06-24, sections 0–29) + `team/stats.md` (live, 2026-06-24) + `team/profile.md`. Every load-bearing claim tier-tagged: `[platform-official]` / `[well-documented]` / `[marketer-claim]` / `[unverified]`. The hub rule governs all of it: **use the DIRECTION, never quote a marketer MAGNITUDE as fact — backtest every number against his own 300-post history.***

---

## Who he is — the voice (2–3 sentences)
Elijah Sullivan, 21, is an AI/tech + money/business creator at **~102,916 followers (+2,811/30d)** who *ships* real AI software (Artifacial, Infinet, ClipWith, Archetype Index, Cruncrr, FlipOps) — a builder, not a commentator. His voice is **high-energy, plain-spoken, blunt-conviction, direct second-person "you," CAPS for emphasis, emoji-forward**, with hooks built on disbelief/audacity/contrarian framing ("ain't no way…", "we were all just exit liquidity the whole time 😳"); never corporate. **Faith ("Jesus is how") is woven in naturally as a value-system frame — identity, never preached** — and long-form goes raw and testimonial.

---

## Ranked niches — his live data (last 30d, `stats.md`)

| Niche | 30d posts | 30d views | ~views/post | Verdict |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Money / Finance** | 18 | 4,087,522 | **~205k** | **LEAN IN for reach** — owns his single biggest reel (3.95M, 2026-05-28) + a 6-mo 5.6M/215k-shares reel; rides REACH not retention (skip-risk) → apply the money-retention fix |
| **Other** (comedic reaction) | 64 | 1,523,313 | ~24k | **Sleeper** — travels on shares (829k breakout); natural-voice release valve, don't over-engineer |
| **Founder / Business** | 32 | 552,388 | ~17k | **LEAN IN** — his most shareable identity lane ("exit liquidity," 206k) |
| **Motivation / Life** | 44 | 344,171 | **~7.8k** | **DEPRIORITIZE** — was most-posted over 6mo (168×) for lowest return; redeploy this volume |
| **AI / Tech** | 21 | 317,856 | ~15k | **GROW — strongest STRATEGIC niche, his #1 fixable gap** |
| **Child Safety / PSA** | 12 | 154,485 | ~12.9k | Mission + arbitrage bet (beats Motivation/post; ties to Cruncrr) |
| **Faith** | 9 | 82,523 | ~9.2k | Identity anchor — conviction, not metrics |

**The AI/Tech execution gap (his single biggest fixable problem):** AI/Tech is the **#1 niche by 6-month total (19.8M, incl. a 10.0M Feb reel)**, holds attention best (**~17.5s vs Motivation's 8.3s**), and is his **highest save-rate niche (~3.6%)** — yet on equal ~20-post counts it pulls **~13× fewer views than Money** (317,856 vs 4,087,522). The gap is **execution, not positioning** — it's his identity moat *and* feeds his ventures. Fix: steal the screen-record-build format + ADD his face + a money/contrarian hook ("Everyone's paying $X for this AI tool — I built it in an afternoon"). `[platform-official, from stats.md]`

---

## What wins for HIM (proven mechanics — share/watch-driven)

- **SHARES are the engine, not likes.** Top reels pull **100k–215k shares**. Every concept should end on an implicit "send this to ___." `[well-documented, from his captions]`
- **Disbelief / audacity / contrarian hook in the first 3s** — his verified winners already do this ("ain't no way…", "the audacity of this fool", "exit liquidity"). **Systematize this pattern across ALL niches, especially AI/builder where it's currently absent.**
- **Outcome-first hook on AI demos** — open frame 1 on the finished result, THEN reveal the how. **#1 data-backed hook type in the verified 34,635-clip OpusClip study (~2× the lowest-ranked).** `[well-documented]`
- **Mute-survival:** ~half of IG video is watched without sound `[platform-official]` → burn the value-promise into on-screen text in frame 1.
- **The curiosity loop** (unresolved question in first 3s, payoff only at the end) — cheapest watch-time lever (the #1 signal).
- **5-hook menu** (not a leaderboard): Outcome Showcase · Expert Explainer · Contrarian · Specific Odd Number · Imperative. A/B against his own history. `[well-documented]`
- **His un-copyable long-form assets:** the **age-14 testimony** (sensitivity-gated — drug/police-beating + self-harm detail is HIS call), the **"7 bets that looked stupid"** arc, **"build software FOR agents, not people"** ("I'll go all-in on AI, I won't put it in my body"), and **build-in-public of his 4 SaaS products** (no money-creator can copy — most have none).

**The 2026 signal model (the spine — every idea is a vehicle for one of these):** Instagram's own "Ranking Explained" names the top Reels predictions as **reshare/send, watch-all-the-way-through, like, audio-page tap**; for Explore/non-follower discovery: **likes, saves, shares**; the named NEGATIVE predictor is **watching <3s**. Mosseri's three to watch: **watch time (#1), sends-per-reach, likes-per-reach** (likes weight more for FOLLOWERS, sends for NON-followers). Sends are "the most heavily weighted signal for distribution." `[platform-official]` → **Score on sends/reach, watch-time, saves/reach — NOT raw likes/views.** Every specific multiplier ("sends 3–5× a like," "saves 3×," "1.4× carousel reach") is `[marketer-claim]` — direction only.

---

## Format strategy

**Recommended mix:** ~**60–70% Reels** · ~**20–30% carousels** · **Stories daily** · **1 long-form/week**. He's at **~6.7 posts/week with 0% carousels** — over-supplying reels, skipping the entire save/convert engine.

- **Reels = the discovery/REACH engine.** Default top-of-funnel; reels get **1.36× the reach of carousels, 2.25× of single images** (Buffer) and **4× the interactions of single images** (Metricool) `[well-documented]`. Up to **3 min now recommended to non-followers, gated by RETENTION not length** `[platform-official]` — unlocks 60–120s mini-explainers (repurpose the Derwin podcast via auto-clip). Eligibility gate: original, no third-party watermarks, has audio, ≤3min, quality, not aggregated `[platform-official]` — **his auto-clip pipeline must enforce per-clip distinctiveness** so near-dupe slices don't read as aggregated. (NOTE: "9:16 required" and "3s hook" are NOT formal gates — 9:16 is export spec, the 3s hook is best-practice.)

- **Carousels = the SAVES/convert engine, and his #1 UNTAPPED LEVER.** He runs **ZERO carousels today** = pure upside; `carousel-builder` has never run in production. Carousels = **9× the saves of single images** (Metricool), **2.09× the engagement rate of reels** + highest median ER-of-reach (6.90% vs reels 3.31%) (Buffer); at 100k–1M accounts they **out-VIEW reels** (Socialinsider), partly because IG re-serves unseen slides as "new content" `[well-documented]`. Explore weights **saves + shares** `[platform-official]` — AI/money reference decks hit two named signals at once. **Build spec:** 8–12 slides, slide 1 = scroll-stopping cover (<12 words), slide 2 = credibility/roadmap, slides 3+ = one point each (design every slide to stand alone — the re-serve resumes from any unseen slide), final = CTA; portrait **1080×1350**, dual **save + send CTA**. Hook devices: **half-reveal across the 1→2 seam, open-loop payoff, progress indicator.** High-save formats (Krumzi): step-by-step · "things I wish I knew" · **Myth vs Fact** · cheat-sheets. **First move: turn the 3.95M "exit liquidity" reel into a saveable AI+money breakdown deck — zero new filming.**

- **Long-form / YouTube = where loyalty + CPM live (the active pivot).** YouTube is the #1 US streaming-watchtime platform ~3 yrs running; paid $100B+ to creators; 2026 = TV-screen, episodic, long-form `[platform-official]`. His lane sits in **top CPM tiers** (AI finance $15–25, AI tutorials/reviews $10–28); finance long-form RPM **~$10–25/1k after the 45% cut vs ~$0.03–0.10 RPM as a Short** — an order-of-magnitude gap and the core staffing argument `[well-documented]`. **Avoid the faceless TTS floor ($4–12) — it's also a YouTube demonetization risk.** Packaging is the gate: **title-first THEN script**, <60 chars, evergreen/undated, first-30s is a hard retention gate `[platform-official]`. **Per-video reach** (a new low-sub channel is NOT gated — every video earns its own reach). His face + live tool demos ARE the moat. *(Never relay the "April 2026 YouTube killed watch-time / 3-min beats 20-min" framing — that's a `[marketer-claim]` dramatization, not official.)*

- **Stories = warm-lead/reply engine, NOT a reach play** (daily, light): poll/Q&A/quiz stickers to manufacture the reply signal, route to DMs.
- **Static single images = AVOID standalone** (Metricool single-image YoY: reach −22%, interactions −25%) — wrap any static asset as carousel slide 1.

---

## Verified trending THEMES worth riding right now (with tiers)

*All fit his AI-builder + money positioning (he owns the credible middle vs the hype crowd) and have built-in shareability. Reactive items go stale — re-pull weekly.*

1. **"Why did the US government turn OFF the best AI model?"** — Claude Fable 5 / Mythos 5 suspended worldwide June 12 2026 under a Commerce export-control directive; Anthropic pulled both and publicly disagreed; Opus 4.8 unaffected `[platform-official]`. Angle: *depending on one AI model is now a business risk.* (Skip the unverified "100+ experts petitioned" / "SK Telecom" sub-claims.)
2. **"GitHub Copilot just 10×–50×'d people's bills overnight"** — usage-based token billing June 1 2026; real bills jumped $29→$750, $50→$3,000 `[platform-official]`. **Load-bearing caveat: code completions stay FREE — only chat/agent/review meter.**
3. **"AI isn't taking your job — here's what's actually happening"** (contrarian myth-bust) — lead with the firm **~0.4% actual AI-attributable role loss** (750-CFO survey) + 60%-of-managers numbers; Altman/Andreessen both call AI a layoff "excuse" `[well-documented]`. **Under-saturated contrarian seat.** (Avoid the deleted 123k/50k/14% specifics.)
4. **The AI bubble / "circular financing" debate** — Burry called the Nvidia→Valor→xAI loop "fugazi"; SEC filing confirms Anthropic pays xAI ~$1.25B/mo `[well-documented]`. **CRITICAL: Burry is NOT alleging fraud — "here's the deal structure, you decide," never "it's fraud."** Pair with verified concentration + the Fed June-17 removal of its 2026 rate-cut. (Don't use the deleted "83% of gains" / "SOX +70%" stats.)
5. **"Sora is dead — here's what I actually use now"** — OpenAI killed the consumer Sora app (~Apr 2026). **Be accurate: Grok Imagine 1.5 is the BUDGET pick (~$4.20/min); Veo 3.1 is PREMIUM (4K+audio, ~$9–24/min); LTX-2.3 open-weights** `[well-documented]`.
6. **"Your AI coding agent can be hijacked" — agentjacking** — malicious instructions in fake Sentry reports executed by Claude Code/Cursor; ~85% test success; **2,388 = DSNs that accepted injected events, NOT 2,388 breaches** `[well-documented]`. Near-zero competition = pure authority builder.
7. **"I priced out running a business on AI agents for a month"** — the $300–500/mo solo-operator stack vs the salaries it replaces `[marketer-claim on the dollars]`. **Show HIS own stack; caveat inflated ROI on-screen (drop the "340%").** Most on-brand money angle he has.
8. **Talent-war + market-shift combo** — Shazeer left Google for OpenAI; **ChatGPT fell below 50% AI-assistant share (46.4%; Gemini 27.7%, Claude 10.3%)**; SpaceX bought Anysphere for $60B `[well-documented]`. **Keep honest: ChatGPT still leads at 1B MAU.**
9. **"You're overpaying for AI" — open models** — GLM-5.2 (MIT-licensed, ~62.1 vs GPT-5.5's 58.6 on SWE-Bench Pro) `[well-documented]`. **Authority hook: "MIT named generative/AI coding a 2026 Breakthrough Technology"** `[platform-official]` — NOT "vibe coding" (that's Karpathy's colloquial term). Verify the exact cost multiple before stating it on camera.
10. **The named money-trend block** — "No-Buy 2026" / "little-treat crackdown," grounded in verified Intuit (59% cutting small purchases, 54% have 2025 regrets) `[well-documented]` + BofA shame/accountability data (41% weekly money guilt, 42% paycheck-to-paycheck) `[platform-official]`. Make it ownable: *"I automated my no-buy with an AI spend-tracker."*

**Recurring series to own** (turns "what do I post" into filling buckets): **"The Model Flood"** (weekly "what dropped + which model wins for [task]") · **"X Clearly Explained (and how to use it)"** (Isenberg's confirmed highest-frequency format `[well-documented]`) · **"AI Builder's 6-Pack"** (Isenberg's fixed-slot digest as a carousel) · **"Building [an AI tool] from $0"** numbered series · **"AI Slop Teardown" / "Grade My Software"** (the `live-software-review` rubric — rides anti-slop sentiment).

---

## Competitive whitespace (the seat nobody owns)
No creator at scale occupies his **triple-intersection — young/Gen-Z + actually-ships-AI-software + money/contrarian operator — and faith is a 4th differentiator none carry.** The field splits cleanly: Riley Brown owns AI-builder (no money/contrarian/faith); Codie/Iman own money (no software, polished-not-raw); AI-agent educators (Berman et al.) are YouTube-primary and weak on IG — **he already out-scales them there.** Position every reel at the overlap: *"I built [real AI feature] this week — here's the money math."* The problem is execution, not positioning. `[well-documented]`

---

## Hard constraints (non-negotiable — govern every output)

- **NEVER publish, post, comment, or DM on any platform without explicit per-action confirmation.** Drafts and container-creation are fine; the final publish click is always Elijah's. This run is **DRAFT-ONLY** — never auto-posts, never creates/edits/pauses an ad, never spends. **Meta Ads = read/report freely, confirm before ANY write.**
- **API-published reels CANNOT use licensed/trending audio** `[platform-official]` → API-publish **only voice-only / original-audio**; any trend-audio reel goes out manually / in-app. *(Caveat: only BUSINESS accounts are restricted to the Meta Sound Collection — verify whether @elijahaifl is Business vs Creator.)* Treat original audio as **OFFENSE** — audio-page taps are a named Reels signal, so a voice-only reel mints a named, tappable audio asset rather than forfeiting the lever.
- **Tier EVERY claim; never relay a marketer MAGNITUDE as fact.** The 3–5× sends weight, 1.4× carousel reach, 70% completion thresholds, "65% more plays from custom thumbnails," "30%/2× keyword captions" — all `[marketer-claim]` or deleted. Use the direction; backtest the number on his own 300-post history (`metrics2026.py` + `self-improve/grade.py`).
- **Avoid hashtag clumps** (−31.70% views / −33.89% interactions vs platform avg `[well-documented]`) — use a question in the caption instead (+36.70% comments). He is NOT hashtag-heavy (max 5, median 5); his real lever is caption **SUBSTANCE** (word-count ~ reach +0.42, first-party recomputed).
- **No faceless/AI-only content with no human** — the actual penalty trigger on both Meta (deception) and YouTube (inauthentic). AI for craft/b-roll/ideation, **NEVER AI for fake proof.** The @elijahaifl handle pre-discloses AI, so he must actively buy trust back: real-face cold open, lead with on-screen receipts (real dashboards/Stripe/insights).
- **FTC disclosure is LAW** for any sponsored/affiliate content — spoken + on-screen, near the FRONT of the caption; the IG Paid Partnership label alone does NOT satisfy it. `[platform-official]`
- **Vault notes follow the property contract** in `obsidian/Elijah's vault/CLAUDE.md` exactly (lowercase snake_case, copy the matching `_templates/` block, never invent names; idea notes → `20-Content/Ideas/`). **Sensitivity gate** on the age-14 testimony: drug/police-beating + self-harm detail is HIS call.

*Note: `profile.md` line 9 still says "~99.7k followers"; live `stats.md` = 102,916. Use the live number.*

ideas.format: Carousel — 8 slides, 1080×1350, each slide built to stand alone (because the re-serve resumes from any unseen slide), swipe-arrow cues, final slide dual save+send CTA.
ideas.why: Socialinsider: at 100k–1M accounts carousels out-VIEW reels partly because IG re-serves unseen slides as 'new content,' resuming from the first slide the viewer missed [well-documented, §73]. He runs ZERO carousels — his #1 untapped lever (§70). A carousel that EXPLAINS the re-serve mechanic is self-demonstrating, hits the saves + sends Explore signals at once, and teaches the exact format he should be shipping. Stand-alone-slide design is the build spec, not decoration.

ideas.title: Watching <3s (the named negative signal, shown as a graveyard)
ideas.hook: Frame 1: "There's ONE thing that tells the algorithm to bury you." → cut to a row of reels each dying at the 2-second mark.
ideas.format: Reel — voice-only, ~12s, curiosity-loop: the unresolved 'one thing' opens it, the <3s payoff lands only at the end. Silent counters tick 0→2s then flatline on each buried reel.
ideas.why: Instagram's own Ranking Explained names watching-<3s as the NEGATIVE predictor [platform-official, brief]. Curiosity loop = the cheapest watch-time lever (#1 signal). The hook deliberately makes YOU not skip in the first 3s to learn why skipping in the first 3s kills you — the format enacts its own lesson. Distinct from prior skip-rate ideas: this is the platform-named negative signal, not his reels_skip_rate column.

ideas.title: The Free Tier That Wasn't (Copilot, as a vanishing 'FREE' stamp)
ideas.hook: Frame 1: "This said FREE on June 1st." — a giant green FREE stamp on a Copilot bill that peels off to reveal $750.
ideas.format: Reel — voice-only, ~14s, single continuous shot of a number climbing $29→$750 as the FREE stamp dissolves; on-screen asterisk persists: 'code completions STILL free — chat/agent/review now meter.'
ideas.why: GitHub usage-based token billing June 1 2026, real bills $29→$750 / $50→$3,000 [platform-official, theme #2]. The load-bearing caveat (completions stay free, only chat/agent/review meter) is burned into on-screen text so the reel can't be 'well actually'd. Distinct from the prior 'Copilot Bill Glow-Up' and 'Free vs Metered' titles: this one weaponizes the FALSE sense of 'free' as the visual betrayal, not a column comparison or a number climb alone. Send-bait: 'send to the dev still on the free plan.'

ideas.title: Agentjacking, Re-enacted (a fake report, executed as a trap)
ideas.hook: Frame 1: "Your coding agent just read a bug report. The bug report read it BACK." — a Sentry-style report opening, its text quietly typing commands into a terminal.
ideas.format: Reel — voice-only, ~18s, screen-record reenactment: the injected instruction in a fake report runs in Claude Code/Cursor; on-screen text corrects the stat live: '2,388 = DSNs that ACCEPTED injected events, NOT 2,388 breaches.'
ideas.why: Agentjacking — malicious instructions in fake Sentry reports executed by coding agents, ~85% test success [well-documented, theme #6]. Near-zero competition = pure authority builder, exactly his AI-builder moat (he ships ClipWith/Artifacial). Distinct from prior 'POV / Fake Bug Report / Sentry Report' titles: this one foregrounds the precise 2,388 correction on-screen as the credibility play — the silent honesty IS the differentiator from the hype crowd. Saves-bait for devs.

ideas.title: $1.5k for a Reel (the silent rate-card receipt)

This is un-copyable lived experience (the Dreamina deal, the Publicis scam) — money-creator whitespace none of the polished gurus have because it's HIS receipts. Founder/business is his most shareable identity lane (§ exit-liquidity 206k). Send-bait: 'send to the creator about to get scammed.' FTC/disclosure not triggered — it's about deals, not a sponsored post.

ideas.title: The Order You Fix Things In: Broad-to-Fine Is a Life Skill, Not Just a Color Trick
ideas.hook: Amateurs fix the tiny stuff first and wonder why nothing improves. Every pro — colorist, builder, writer — works broad to fine. Here's the order.
ideas.format: Carousel, 9 slides. Slide 1 = cover (broad-to-fine as a universal order). Slides 2-8 = the principle ported across domains (grading a video, debugging code, editing a script, fixing your finances) — each slide = one domain, same order. Slide 9 = save this mental model.
ideas.why: Takes the consensus broad-to-fine methodology from his own color-grading knowledge and abstracts it into a portable mental model — exactly the 'framework' lane with no product. Cross-domain application makes every slide stand alone (resume-from-anywhere), and 'cheat-sheet / mental model' is a high-save format. Demonstrates builder credibility without a CTA.

ideas.title: Two Audiences, Two Buttons: Why Your Followers Hit Like and Strangers Hit Send
ideas.hook: Likes weight more for the people who already follow you. Sends weight more for the strangers IG is deciding whether to show you to. You're optimizing for the wrong room.
ideas.format: Carousel, 8 slides. Slide 1 = the two-button reveal. Slides 2-7 = the follower-vs-non-follower signal split, why a 'safe' post pleases followers but never escapes, how to build the send trigger into the last 3 seconds, the curiosity-loop tie-in. Slide 8 = send-this CTA (meta payoff).
ideas.why: Grounded in the [platform-official] Mosseri detail that likes weight more for followers and sends for non-followers — a precise, under-explained mechanic. It's a fresh CAROUSEL treatment (prior items on this were reels/different framing) and the final slide weaponizes the exact signal it teaches. No product, pure distribution literacy.

ideas.title: The 3-Minute Unlock: Length Stopped Mattering — Retention Started
ideas.hook: Instagram now pushes 3-minute reels to people who don't follow you. The catch nobody mentions: length is no longer the gate. Retention is.
ideas.format: Carousel, 9 slides. Slide 1 = the unlock + the catch. Slides 2-8 = what 'retention-gated not length-gated' actually means, the per-clip distinctiveness rule so slices don't read as 'aggregated', how a 90s mini-explainer survives, the eligibility checklist (original, has audio, no third-party watermark, quality). Slide 9 = save the checklist.
ideas.why: Built on the [platform-official] shift: up to 3 min recommended to non-followers, gated by retention not length, plus the named eligibility gates. Corrects the common misread that 'longer = more reach.' It's an actionable checklist (high-save), strictly mechanics with no product, and quietly supports his long-form/Derwin-repurpose pivot. The 'not aggregated' caveat is the load-bearing detail most creators miss.

ideas.title: I Tracked 300 Of My Own Posts. The Algorithm Doesn't Reward What You Think.
ideas.hook: SLIDE 1 (cover, <12 words): "I posted 300 times. The data killed everything I believed about IG."
ideas.format: Carousel, 9 slides, 1080x1350. Slide 1 = cover (myth-killer promise). Slide 2 = credibility/roadmap ("3 beliefs the data destroyed, receipts on every slide"). Slides 3-7 = ONE debunked belief per slide as a standalone mini-cover: 'Likes don't move you' / 'Posting daily ≠ more reach per post' / 'Motivation was my biggest niche AND my poorest' / 'My AI content has the BEST retention and the WORST views' / 'Carousels beat reels and I ran zero'. Slide 8 = the one real lever (sends + watch-time). Slide 9 = dual CTA (save + 'send this to the creator still chasing likes').
ideas.why: Hits the Explore-named saves+shares signals at once (Myth vs Fact is a verified top-4 save format, Krumzi). Grounded ENTIRELY in his own 300-post history (the hub's only ground truth) so nothing is a marketer magnitude. The self-audit angle is un-copyable proof-of-builder — no rival shows their own back-end data on a slide.

ideas.title: Everyone Told Me To Niche Down. I Run 4 Companies On Purpose.
ideas.hook: SLIDE 1 (cover): "'Pick ONE thing' is the most expensive advice in business. Here's the math."
ideas.format: Carousel, 10 slides. Half-reveal across the 1→2 seam ('Pick ONE thing' on slide 1 -> '...is how you build ONE point of failure' on slide 2). Slides 3-8 = one company per slide (Artifacial / Infinet / ClipWith / Archetype) framed as 'what each one taught the next' — the portfolio as a compounding system, not scatter. Slide 9 = the contrarian thesis ('leverage across bets beats betting the farm'). Slide 10 = 'Save this for the next time someone tells you to niche down' + send CTA.
ideas.why: Founder/Business is his most shareable identity lane (~18k/post, the 'exit liquidity' breakout). Build-in-public of his 4 real SaaS products is the competitive whitespace NO money-creator can copy (most have zero products). Contrarian-take-against-conventional-wisdom is his verified winning hook pattern, applied to the builder seat where it's currently absent.

ideas.title: The Best AI Model Got Turned Off By The Government. Nobody Asked The Real Question.
ideas.hook: SLIDE 1 (cover): "A model millions relied on vanished overnight — by GOVERNMENT order. Read slide 7."
ideas.format: Carousel, 8 slides. Slide 1 = cover with an open loop pointing to a later slide (verified curiosity-loop device). Slide 2 = the verified spine (Claude Fable 5 / Mythos 5 suspended worldwide June 12 2026 under a Commerce export-control directive; Anthropic pulled both and publicly disagreed; Opus 4.8 unaffected). Slides 3-6 = one consequence per slide ('your workflow had a single point of failure you didn't choose' / 'pricing & access aren't yours to control' / 'export rules now reach into your stack' / 'this will happen again'). Slide 7 = the real question ('what runs if your model disappears tomorrow?'). Slide 8 = 'Save this before you build your whole business on one API' + send CTA.
ideas.why: Verified [platform-official] theme #1 from the brief, reframed as a contrarian story (the real lesson nobody's drawing). AI/Tech is his #1 fixable gap and highest-save niche (~3.6%) — a reference deck on a real news event hits saves+shares (named Explore signals). Sticks to the verified spine, drops the unverified petition/SK-Telecom sub-claims per the brief.

ideas.title: I Don't Trust Any Result I Can't Put On My Own Screen
ideas.hook: SLIDE 1 (cover): "My handle literally says 'AI.' Here's the rule that makes you trust me anyway."
ideas.format: Carousel, 8 slides. Slide 1 = cover (pattern-interrupt: owns the trust problem out loud). Slide 2 = the credo ('AI helps me MAKE it — it doesn't FAKE it'). Slides 3-6 = one principle per slide: 'real face before any AI b-roll' / 'lead with real dashboards, real Stripe, real insights' / 'AI for craft, never for fake proof' / 'if I can't substantiate it, it doesn't go on a slide'. Slide 7 = the line in the sand ('the penalty isn't using AI — it's deception'). Slide 8 = 'Save this as your authenticity filter' + 'send to anyone drowning in AI slop'.
ideas.why: Turns the @elijahaifl handle's disclosed-AI trust penalty into a moat (state-of-play §11). Anti-slop sentiment is documented and rising; the authenticity premium is verified [well-documented]. Faith-adjacent value-system framing ('I build differently') is his un-copyable 4th differentiator. Carousel = the save engine for a manifesto people screenshot.

ideas.title: Your AI Subscription Is The New Cable Bill. I Cut Mine In Half.
ideas.hook: SLIDE 1 (cover): "You're stacking AI subscriptions like a 2010 cable package. Here's the cut list."
ideas.format: Carousel, 10 slides. Slide 1 = cover (relatable contrarian frame). Slide 2 = the thesis ('most of your AI spend is overlap you forgot to cancel'). Slides 3-8 = one swap/cut per slide as a standalone cheat-card: open-model swap (GLM-5.2, MIT-licensed, ~62.1 vs GPT-5.5's 58.6 SWE-Bench Pro), the GitHub Copilot meter caveat ('completions are FREE — only chat/agent/review bill you'), the Sora-killed video swap (Grok Imagine = budget, Veo = premium), 'one tool doing two jobs', 'free tier covers 80% of you'. Slide 9 = the honest caveat ('verify the exact cost multiple on YOUR usage — I did'). Slide 10 = 'Save this audit' + 'send to whoever pays the team's AI bills'.
ideas.why: Marries the money niche (his reach leader, ~205k/post) with AI/Tech (his strategic gap) at the exact triple-intersection nobody owns. Cheat-sheet is a verified top-save format (Krumzi). Rides verified themes #2/#5/#9. The on-slide 'verify on your own usage' caveat enforces the hub's no-marketer-magnitude rule visibly, which itself builds trust.

ideas.title: I Looked Stupid In Public 7 Times. Slide 6 Is The One That Paid.
ideas.hook: SLIDE 1 (cover): "7 things people roasted me for. Every single one was right. Swipe."
ideas.format: Carousel, 9 slides. Slide 1 = cover with a forward open loop ('slide 6'). Slide 2 = the frame ('looking stupid in public is the cheapest tuition there is'). Slides 3-8 = one 'stupid' bet per slide, each as a standalone story-card (e.g. 'AI before it was cool' / 'building for agents not people' / 'credit & leverage over cash' / 'four companies not one' / 'faith on a money page' / 'shipping ugly v1s in public'). Slide 6 = the highest-payoff one (delivers on the cover's promise). Slide 9 = 'Save this for the next time you're scared to look dumb' + send CTA.
ideas.why: Built on his verified un-copyable '7 bets that looked stupid' arc but as a SAVEABLE deck, not a reel (distinct from the existing reel-titled versions). 'Things I wish I knew' / numbered-story is a verified top-save carousel format. Forward open loop + progress-revealing structure = verified curiosity-loop retention device for swipe-completion. Story+conviction is his testimonial long-form voice compressed into the save engine.

ideas.title: Spending Shame Is A Business Model. You're The Product.
ideas.hook: SLIDE 1 (cover): "41% of people feel guilty about money every week. That guilt is being SOLD."
ideas.format: Carousel, 9 slides. Slide 1 = cover (verified BofA stat as the audacity hook). Slide 2 = the contrarian flip ('the 'discipline' content keeping you broke-minded is its own funnel'). Slides 3-7 = one reframe per slide: 'no-buy challenges sell you the tracker' / 'the real numbers (Intuit: 59% cutting small buys, 54% have regrets)' / 'guilt ≠ a plan' / 'small purchases aren't why you're broke' / 'build the system, skip the shame'. Slide 8 = 'I automated mine instead of shaming myself' (his ownable angle). Slide 9 = 'Save this before your next no-buy guilt spiral' + 'send to a friend who budgets out of fear'.
ideas.why: Money niche (his reach leader) with a verified contrarian seat — built on [platform-official] BofA data (41% weekly guilt, 42% paycheck-to-paycheck) and [well-documented] Intuit numbers, not marketer math. Emotional myth-bust triggers shares (verified Explore signal). Distinct from the existing 'No-Buy 2026 is shame in a trench coat' title by going after the SHAME-AS-FUNNEL mechanism, not the trend itself.

ideas.title: The Smartest People In AI Build It. The Richest People Distribute It.
ideas.hook: SLIDE 1 (cover): "You don't need to build the model. You need to own the distribution. Here's why."
ideas.format: Carousel, 9 slides. Slide 1 = cover (contrarian thesis stated flat). Slide 2 = the proof setup (the model layer is commoditizing — open weights match paid frontier; ChatGPT fell below 50% AI-assistant share at 46.4%). Slides 3-7 = one principle per slide: 'the model is a feature, not a moat' / 'distribution is the durable asset' / 'a face + real demos can't be cloned' / 'audience > algorithm (own a list)' / 'pick the wave, don't build the ocean'. Slide 8 = his receipt ('I ship software AND distribution — that's the whole edge'). Slide 9 = 'Save this if you keep waiting to 'learn to code' first' + send CTA.
ideas.why: Pure contrarian POV deck at his exact triple-intersection (ships-software + money-operator + young). Grounded in verified theme #8 (ChatGPT <50%, 46.4%) and the open-model commoditization (theme #9), direction-only per the hub rule. Reframes the AI-builder conversation toward distribution — his actual moat (face + 4 products + audience) — which is the competitive whitespace no rival occupies. High shareability (a thesis builders argue about = comments + sends).

ideas.title: I Asked 4 AI Models the Same Money Question — One Answer Could Get You Sued
ideas.hook: Frame 1 on-screen text over his face: "I asked GPT, Gemini, Claude + a free one for tax advice. Watch what happened." Spoken: "Ain't no way one of these told me to do something ILLEGAL with a straight face..."
ideas.format: 30-45s Reel, talking-to-camera with split-screen phone screen-record of the 4 answers, outcome teased frame 1
ideas.why: Outcome-first hook (the #1 ranked OpusClip hook, ~2x lowest) + curiosity loop held to the reveal = the cheapest watch-time lever (#1 signal). Sits dead-center of his AI/Tech-meets-Money whitespace and forces the implicit 'send this to anyone using AI for money' = sends (most-weighted distribution signal). Fixes the AI/Tech execution gap by bolting a money/contrarian hook onto a screen-record-build.

ideas.title: Everyone's Building 'AI Agents' — I Tested If Mine Could Survive a Trap I Set
ideas.hook: "I left a poisoned bug report in my own codebase to see if my AI agent would fall for it. The audacity of what it did next 😳"
ideas.format: 45-60s Reel, face cold-open then screen-record of the agent reading the injected instruction, real terminal receipts
ideas.why: Rides the verified agentjacking theme (near-zero competition = pure authority builder) but as a HIM-does-it experiment, not news relay — un-copyable because he actually ships agent software. Real-face + on-screen receipts buys back trust the @elijahaifl handle pre-spends. 'audacity' is a verified winning hook pattern in his own captions.

ideas.title: The 19 Million Views I Got Last Year Came From a Niche I Barely Post In
ideas.hook: On-screen frame 1: "My #1 niche over 6 months: 19.8M views. I post it the LEAST. Here's why I'm broke on purpose." Spoken: "We were all just sleeping on the one thing that actually works..."
ideas.format: 30s Reel, talking-to-camera, his real analytics dashboard on screen as the receipt
ideas.why: Build-in-public meta-transparency none of his money/AI competitors can copy (most have no real account data to show). Contrarian 'I'm doing it wrong on purpose' hook + a specific odd number (19.8M) hits two of the 5-hook menu at once. Dashboard-on-screen = real receipt, the trust-buy his handle requires. Sets up his own AI/Tech pivot narratively.

ideas.title: I Let an AI Decide What I Bought for a Week — It Was Cheaper Than Me AND a Snob
ideas.hook: "I gave my AI veto power over every purchase for 7 days. It rejected my coffee. Then it bought something I'd NEVER buy 😭"
ideas.format: 45-60s Reel, vlog-style talking segments + receipt B-roll, the unexpected purchase as the payoff
ideas.why: Ties the verified No-Buy 2026 / little-treat-crackdown money trend (Intuit 59% cutting small purchases) to his own Cruncrr build = ownable angle the brief flagged. Comedic-reaction energy = his 'Other' sleeper niche that travels on shares (829k breakout). Open loop ('something I'd never buy') resolved only at the end = watch-time. Distinct from the prior no-buy titles by being an AI-has-veto experiment, not a tracker demo.

ideas.title: A Free AI Model Beat the $200 One — So I Did the Most Honest Test I Could
ideas.hook: Frame 1, the finished score on screen: "FREE model: 62. The $200/mo one: 58. I ran the same job through both. No edits."
ideas.format: 30-45s Reel, outcome-first (score shown frame 1), then face-to-camera explaining the test, side-by-side screen-record
ideas.why: Outcome-first hook (verified #1 type) with the verified GLM-5.2 vs GPT-5.5 SWE-Bench direction — but brief says VERIFY the exact cost multiple before stating on camera, so the hook leads with the score not an unverified $ multiple. 'You're overpaying for AI' is an under-saturated authority seat. Save-bait (he keeps the comparison) = saves/reach signal. Distinct from prior free-model title by being a live head-to-head test, not the MIT-named framing.

ideas.title: I Read Every Word of GitHub's New Pricing — Here's the One Setting That Saves You $700
ideas.hook: "Everyone's panicking about $750 Copilot bills. The fix is ONE toggle. Send this before your invoice hits."
ideas.format: 30s Reel, talking-to-camera + screen-record of the exact settings toggle
ideas.why: Rides the verified Copilot usage-billing theme but as an actionable fix (the load-bearing caveat: completions stay FREE, only chat/agent/review meter) — turns a fear-news cycle into a save-and-send asset. Explicit 'send this before your invoice' = direct sends CTA (most-weighted non-follower signal). Distinct from the prior '50x'd bills' shock title by being the practical de-escalation seat nobody owns.

ideas.title: My Faith Is Why I'll Build AI Agents but Never an AI Girlfriend
ideas.hook: "People keep asking why I won't touch the most profitable corner of AI. It's not a business reason. Let me be real with you for a second."
ideas.format: 45-60s Reel, raw talking-to-camera, single take, no captions gimmickry — testimonial register
ideas.why: Faith-as-value-system woven naturally (identity, never preached) = his 4th differentiator NO competitor at scale carries. Conviction-over-metrics, but the contrarian 'I'm leaving money on the table on purpose' framing makes it shareable, not preachy. Long-form-raw register in short form. Distinct from prior faith/AI-in-my-body titles by drawing a specific business line (the companion-AI category) rooted in the value frame.

ideas.title: I Built a Tool to Catch Predators in Roblox DMs in a Weekend — Big Tech Won't
ideas.hook: On-screen frame 1: "It took me a weekend. They have 10,000 engineers. Why is MY kid-safety tool better than theirs?" Spoken, low and direct: "This one's not for the views."
ideas.format: 45-60s Reel, face-to-camera + restrained screen-record of the detection working on a sample, no hype edit
ideas.why: Child-Safety/PSA is his mission + arbitrage bet (beats Motivation per post, ties to Cruncrr) and an un-copyable builder asset. Contrarian-audacity hook ('a weekend vs 10,000 engineers') = his verified pattern, aimed at the most shareable emotion (protect-your-kid). Real working demo = the receipt that buys trust. The 'not for the views' line signals authenticity and paradoxically earns shares. Distinct lane — no prior title touches child-safety.

ideas.title: The Two Receipts (a salary slip vs an API bill, side by side)
ideas.hook: ON-SCREEN FRAME 1: "This replaced this." — a $5,400 paystub fading into a $312 Anthropic/OpenAI invoice.
ideas.format: Silent split-screen reel: left half a redacted monthly salary slip, right half his real monthly AI-agent invoice. A single hand swaps one for the other; on-screen text tallies the gap ("one human role → $312/mo of agents"). Original audio = paper rustle + a register cha-ching. 12-18s. Faceless except his hand.
ideas.why: Money/Finance is his runaway reach leader (~205k views/post) and the §4 theme #7 "priced out running a business on AI agents" is his most on-brand money angle. Outcome-first visual receipt (a real bill) is the scarce trust signal §11 says slop can't fake, and the implicit "send this to whoever's hiring" hits the sends-per-reach engine. Caveat the ROI honestly on-screen, no inflated multiplier.

ideas.title: Same Prompt, Six Bills (the model-cost ladder, climbing on its own)
ideas.hook: ON-SCREEN FRAME 1: "I ran the EXACT same task on 6 models. Watch the price."
ideas.format: Silent kinetic-type reel: one identical task prompt at top, then six price tags stack and climb in sequence (GLM-5.2 free/MIT → ... → the premium tier), each snapping in with a tick SFX, the cheapest open-weight one ringed in green at the end. No face, pure on-screen numbers. 15-20s.
ideas.why: Rides §4 theme #9 "you're overpaying for AI" (GLM-5.2 MIT-licensed) without quoting an unverified multiple — the climbing ladder shows direction, and the open-loop "watch the price" is the curiosity loop (the #1 watch-time lever). AI/Tech is his highest save-rate niche (~3.6%); a price-comparison reference frame is inherently save-worthy. Verify exact per-token numbers before any specific figure goes on screen.

ideas.title: The 30-Second Tea Test (your savings account, losing the race)
ideas.hook: ON-SCREEN FRAME 1: "Your 'high-yield' savings vs inflation. 30 seconds. Loser leaves."
ideas.format: Silent show-don't-tell race-bar reel: two animated progress bars labeled SAVINGS APY and INFLATION; the inflation bar quietly overtakes savings while a held dollar-figure chunk ticks down. No talking, no face. End card: "send this to whoever still keeps it all in savings." 18-22s.
ideas.why: Applies the §10 Money-retention fix verbatim — open-loop with a number/promise in the first ~2.5s, single clean payoff, 2-4 word held caption chunks for the ~70-85% sound-off majority. Money rides reach but leaks watch-time; a visual race holds the curve to the resolution. Explicit send CTA targets sends-per-reach (the named non-follower signal).

ideas.title: One AI Went Dark (a world map losing a light at 00:12)
ideas.hook: ON-SCREEN FRAME 1: "June 12, 2026. The best AI model got switched off worldwide. Watch."
ideas.format: Silent map/dashboard reel: a glowing 'Fable 5 / Mythos 5' status indicator on a stylized world map flips from green to grey at a ticking clock, while every dependent 'app' icon wired to it greys out in a cascade. On-screen text: "if your business ran on ONE model, it just stopped." Faceless. 15-20s.
ideas.why: Rides §4 theme #1 (the verified Commerce export-control suspension, Opus 4.8 unaffected) with the load-bearing lesson 'single-model dependency is a business risk' — the cascade SHOWS the thesis instead of arguing it. Founder/Business is his most shareable identity lane ("exit liquidity" pattern); the cascade is a built-in 'send this to your CTO' trigger. Stays on the verified spine, skips the unverified petition/SK-Telecom sub-claims.

ideas.title: Find the Real One (4 dashboards, 3 are AI slop)
ideas.hook: ON-SCREEN FRAME 1: "3 of these 4 'results' are fake. Can you spot mine? (real one revealed at the end)"
ideas.format: Silent spot-the-difference reel: a 2x2 grid of four near-identical revenue/results dashboards; three subtly wrong (impossible numbers, AI-render tells), one is his actual screen-record. A magnifier hovers; at the end his real one gets a green check while the three slop ones get an X. Faceless until the reveal frame. 20-25s.
ideas.why: Turns the @elijahaifl AI-disclosure liability into the §11 moat — it literally teaches the audience to distrust faceless AI proof while positioning him as the one with REAL on-screen receipts. The 'spot it, answer revealed at the end' is a pure curiosity loop (watch-time #1) AND a comment magnet (guessing in comments). Rides the documented anti-slop sentiment; near-zero competition.

ideas.title: The Co-Founder With No Face (Tanner, captioned like a wildlife subject)
ideas.hook: ON-SCREEN FRAME 1 (nature-doc lower-third style): "The North American Solo-Founder. Habitat: 3 monitors. Predators: their own API bill."
ideas.format: Silent faceless skit: b-roll of Tanner working, captioned ENTIRELY in deadpan nature-documentary lower-thirds (no VO needed — text-on-screen carries it). Each beat a new 'field observation' that lands a real builder-life truth. End: "tag your co-founder in their natural habitat." 18-25s.
ideas.why: The §5 'faceless co-founder / Tanner character series' is already a validated format direction, and the 'Other'/comedic-reaction lane is his sleeper that travels on SHARES (829k breakout). Text-on-screen survives mute (§1, ~half watch sound-off). The 'tag your co-founder' close is a literal send trigger — sends are the most heavily weighted distribution signal. Distinct from the prior nature-doc idea: this one is caption-only, zero VO, observation-skit structure.

ideas.title: What $750 Bought Last Month (the Copilot bill, itemized like a crime scene)
ideas.hook: ON-SCREEN FRAME 1: "This dev's bill went $29 → $750 overnight. Here's the ONE line that did it."
ideas.format: Silent screen-record reel: a real metered-billing statement scrolls; a red marker circles the chat/agent/review line items climbing while 'CODE COMPLETIONS — $0.00 FREE' stays highlighted green and untouched. On-screen text: "completions are still free. THIS is what meters." No face. 15-20s.
ideas.why: Rides §4 theme #2 (verified June-1 usage-based billing, real $29→$750 / $50→$3,000 bills) WITH the load-bearing caveat baked into the visual — completions stay free, only chat/agent/review meter. Relatable money-pain on a builder tool = high comments + saves (it's a 'how not to get wrecked' reference). Itemizing-as-evidence is the on-screen-receipt trust play from §11.

ideas.title: The Tier List That Saves You $20/mo (drag-and-drop, no voice)
ideas.hook: ON-SCREEN FRAME 1: "AI video tools, ranked by what they ACTUALLY cost per minute. Save this before you pay for the wrong one."
ideas.format: Silent drag-and-drop tier-list reel: tool logos slide into S/A/B/F rows keyed to verified per-minute cost (Grok Imagine 1.5 budget ~$4.20/min as the value pick, Veo 3.1 as premium ~$9-24/min, LTX-2.3 open-weights), a hand placing each card. Original audio = card snaps. End: "save this · send it to whoever's still paying for Sora." 20-25s.
ideas.why: Rides §4 theme #5 (Sora app killed; accurate budget-vs-premium framing) and is built as a SAVE asset — a cost reference deck hits saves/reach, a named Explore signal. Dual save+send CTA (§2/§6) targets both Mosseri metrics. Tier-list drag is a proven non-talking format that holds attention through the placement reveals (curiosity loop). Keeps the corrected pricing direction; verify exact $/min before any hard number on screen.

ideas.title: I Asked GitHub Copilot to Build Something — Then Watched the Meter Spin Like a Gas Pump
ideas.hook: On-screen frame 1 (face + screen): "this is what $40 in 6 minutes looks like" — a live token-cost counter ticking up next to the agent running.
ideas.format: Reel, 30–45s, screen-record + face cold open, original/voice audio (API-publishable). Live cost-counter overlay is the visual gimmick.
ideas.why: Theme #2 (Copilot usage-based billing, $29→$750 real bills, June 1 2026) is platform-official money-pain = high comments. Outcome-first visual hook (the meter spinning) is the #1 hook type in the 34,635-clip OpusClip study (~2× lowest). The literal money-on-screen survives mute (~half watch silent). Load-bearing caveat burned on-screen: "completions are still FREE — only agent/chat meters" so he teaches instead of fearmongers → save + send.

ideas.title: The AI 'Job Apocalypse' Is 0.4% — I Did the Math the Headlines Won't
ideas.hook: Frame 1 text over his face: "everyone's scared of the wrong number 📉" then cut to a whiteboard with '0.4%' circled next to a crossed-out '14%'.
ideas.format: Reel, 40–60s, talking-head + one whiteboard b-roll insert, voice/original audio.
ideas.why: Theme #3 is the under-saturated CONTRARIAN seat — his proven hook lane (disbelief/audacity). Leads with the firm 0.4% actual-AI-role-loss figure (750-CFO survey, well-documented) + Altman/Andreessen 'excuse' quotes; avoids the deleted 123k/14% specifics per the brief. Contrarian myth-bust = his single most shareable identity move ('exit liquidity' DNA) → maps to sends-per-reach for non-followers.

ideas.title: A Free MIT-Licensed Model Just Beat GPT-5.5 and Almost Nobody Switched — Watch Me Run Both
ideas.hook: Frame 1 split-screen: two terminals side by side, label burned in: "FREE model vs the $20/mo one — same task, GO".
ideas.format: Reel, 45–60s, dual screen-record race + face reaction, voice/original audio (API-publishable).
ideas.why: Theme #9 (GLM-5.2 MIT-licensed, ~62.1 vs GPT-5.5 58.6 SWE-Bench Pro) + the platform-official authority hook 'MIT named generative coding a 2026 Breakthrough Technology'. Outcome-first head-to-head (finished result frame 1) = top OpusClip hook type. He runs BOTH live = the un-copyable real-demo proof that buys back the @elijahaifl AI-disclosure trust tax. The Specific Odd Number hook (6 words) + Myth-bust energy. Step-by-step + cheat-sheet = top save formats; the reusable test invites the save-for-later behavior carousels are built for.

Slide 9 = save + send CTA.

ideas.title: Why You Finish Things You Can't Pause: The Open-Loop Trap, Explained

ideas.title: Volume Isn't the Flex: Why Posting Less Beat Posting More for Me

Slide 7 = the test to run on yourself. Slide 8 = save + send-to-an-over-poster CTA.

Contrarian hook ('posting less beat posting more') matches his proven disbelief-pattern. Highly send-worthy to other creators = hits the non-follower send signal.

ideas.title: The Authenticity Premium: Why 'Real' Is Now the Rarest Thing You Can Sell

ideas.title: Broad to Fine: The Order You Fix Anything In (Stop Polishing Slide 1)

ideas.title: You Don't Have a Goal Problem, You Have a Frame-1 Problem

'Things nobody tells you' + actionable steps = high-save. Relatable pain (not starting) = high send/share. Redeploys his over-supplied Motivation volume into a SAVEABLE format that actually returns.

ideas.title: The Re-Serve Is Free Distribution: 5 Carousel Moves Reels Can't Do

Hits the named Explore SAVES + SHARES signals directly. Distinct from the prior 'Re-Serve Loophole' reel title by being the actionable 5-move playbook a creator screenshots. Highly send-worthy to other creators = non-follower send signal. Demonstrates the format he himself runs ZERO of today.

ideas.title: I Lost 57% Of My Reach In One Month. The Cause Wasn't My Content — It Was Me.

Half-reveal across 1->2 seam: cover names the drop, slide 2 shows the receipts.

Vulnerability + a number every creator fears = high send/reshare (the #1 distribution signal). Niche: Founder/Business (his most shareable identity lane).

ideas.title: You Don't Have 103,000 Followers. You Have 103,000 Strangers — And That's The Whole Game.

ideas.title: Google Isn't Your Homepage Anymore. An AI You Don't Control Decides If You Exist.

He owns the credible middle (actually ships AI) vs the hype crowd. Niche: AI/Tech (his #1 fixable gap, highest save-rate niche ~3.6%).

ideas.title: My Median Reel Gets 2,400 Views. Yes, The Guy With The 3.95 Million One.

Slide 7 = the reframe (volume of SWINGS, not consistency of average). Slide 8 = save + send CTA ('send this to the friend who quit after 10 flat posts').

ideas.title: The Fed Just Quietly Cancelled Your Rate Cut. Nobody Posted About It Because It's Not Exciting.

ideas.title: I Talk About Jesus On A Money Page. The Engagement Went UP. The Research Explains Why.

Slide 8 = the takeaway (lead with who you are, not what you sell). Slide 9 = save + send CTA.

ideas.title: You're Not Bad With Money. You're Just Allergic To Leverage. There's A Difference.

Slide 8 = the shift (audit your leverage, not your lattes). Slide 9 = save + send CTA.

ideas.title: I Stopped Posting 4 Times a Day — Here's What Happened to My Reach

ideas.title: A Voice-Only Reel Is a Cheat Code Now — and Almost Nobody Realizes Why
ideas.hook: This reel has no trending audio, no music — and that's EXACTLY why Instagram is about to push it harder.
ideas.format: Talking-head, voice-only / original-audio (also makes it API-publishable per §7); on-screen text in frame 1 since it must survive mute; 35-50s; explainer-of-an-overlooked-mechanic.
ideas.why: Turns the audio constraint into OFFENSE: 'go to the audio page' is one of the FOUR named Reels predictions, and original audio he authors mints a named, tappable audio asset (§7, §19, [platform-official]). Under-covered mechanic = whitespace. Mute-survival on-screen text = the ~half-watched-silent rule (§1). Voice-only is the one format he can API-publish safely. Expert-explainer hook from the 5-hook menu.

ideas.title: They Just Paid $60 BILLION for the App That Writes Code — Here's What That Tells You
ideas.hook: SpaceX just spent SIXTY BILLION dollars on a coding app — and if you understand WHY, you understand where all the money's going.
ideas.format: Talking-head, disbelief/audacity hook ('$60 BILLION'), CAPS on the number; on-screen 'largest VC-backed acquisition ever' receipt; 45-60s; explainer that decodes the signal, not just reports it.
ideas.why: Uses the verified SpaceX→Anysphere $60B all-stock deal (§4 #8, [well-documented]) — a fresh angle: NOT the talent-war 'who quit' frame already used, but the 'what the price tag signals about AI's center of gravity' decode. Audacity hook = his 3.95M-pattern. Money + AI overlap = his triple-intersection seat (§9). 'Send this to the friend who thinks AI is a bubble' = sends.

ideas.title: My Whole Audience Could Vanish Tomorrow — So I Built the One Thing Instagram Can't Take
ideas.hook: If Instagram deleted my account tonight, I'd lose 103 thousand followers in one click — so I stopped betting my business on it.
ideas.format: Raw, conviction talking-head (his testimonial register); shows the owned-channel he's building (Telegram/newsletter) on-screen as receipts; 50-70s; vulnerability-into-strategy arc.
ideas.why: Built on the §13 owned-audience hedge (his ~0-owned-audience gap = biggest structural risk) + the §16 June-2026 Meta-AI account-takeover wave — both verified, both genuinely scary and shareable. Faith-adjacent steward framing ('build what survives') fits his un-copyable value-system differentiator (§5) without preaching. 'Tag the creator with zero email list' = sends + comments. High-conviction = his most authentic register.

ideas.title: I Built 4 Software Companies on Borrowed Money — Cash Is the Trap Nobody Warns You About
ideas.hook: I'm 21, I've built four AI companies, and I did almost none of it with my own cash — here's the operator move they don't teach you.
ideas.format: Talking-head, contrarian operator hook; on-screen names of his real ventures (Artifacial/Infinet/ClipWith/Archetype) as proof-of-builder receipts; 45-65s.
ideas.why: Leans on his verified contrarian operator identity ('credit-and-leverage over cash', §5) + build-in-public of 4 real SaaS products NO money-creator can copy (§5, §9) — the literal whitespace seat. Contrarian-money hook in his blunt voice = his proven pattern. Money + Founder + AI-builder triple-overlap in one line (§9). 'Send this to the friend saving up to start' = the sends engine.

ideas.title: I Tell People I'm AI — and It Costs Me Trust on Every Single Video. Here's How I Earn It Back
ideas.hook: My handle literally says 'AI' — so half of you don't trust a word I say. Good. Let me show you every result is real, live, on my screen.
ideas.format: Talking-head credo reel, real-face cold open then a live screen-record of a real dashboard/Stripe/insights (verifiable receipts); 50-70s; the 'I use AI, I'm not made of AI' pinned-credo format.
ideas.why: Directly executes the §11 move #3: a pinned 'I use AI, I am not made of AI' credo reel that converts the @elijahaifl handle's built-in trust penalty (60%→26% disclosed-AI preference) into a moat. Leading with on-screen receipts is both SAFE (no deceptive-AI third rail) and the scarce authenticity premium (§11, §5). Honest-confession hook = pattern-interrupt; identity content = high saves + a values statement people share.

ideas.title: The Petition That Got Ignored
ideas.hook: Slide-1 / frame-1 text on a clean black screen: "They turned off the AI. The company that BUILT it said don't." — then a single timeline bar fills to June 12 and a status light flips from green to red.
ideas.format: Silent kinetic-type reel (15-20s). One status light, one date stamp, one quote card. No face needed beyond a 1s real-face cold-open tag for trust. Original audio = a low tension hum (mints a tappable audio asset).
ideas.why: Rides verified theme #1 (Claude Fable 5 / Mythos 5 suspended worldwide June 12 2026 under a Commerce directive; Anthropic publicly disagreed) [platform-official] but lands a FRESH angle the prior 'Off Switch' / 'One AI Went Dark' titles never used: the builder-vs-government conflict, not the blackout itself. Curiosity-loop (who overruled whom?) resolves only at the end = the #1 watch-time lever. Mute-survival: full value burns into text in frame 1.

ideas.title: Two Lines of Code, Two Prices
ideas.hook: Split screen, no voice. LEFT label: "Code completion → still FREE". RIGHT label: "Chat / agent → now metered". A token counter on the RIGHT spins up while the LEFT stays frozen at $0.
ideas.format: Silent split-screen demo reel (12-18s) — real Copilot UI screen-record on one side, a frozen $0 card on the other. Outcome-first: the two prices are visible in frame 1.
ideas.why: Corrects the single most-missed nuance in theme #2 — usage-based token billing hit June 1 2026 and bills jumped $29→$750, but the load-bearing caveat is code completions stay FREE; only chat/agent/review meter [platform-official]. The prior Copilot titles ('Free vs Metered', 'Code Completions: FREE') stated the asterisk; this one SHOWS the mechanism live (which line bills, which doesn't) = a distinct save-worthy explainer. Saves/reach is a named Explore signal.

ideas.title: Six Bills, One Question
ideas.hook: Frame 1, all six visible at once: a 2×3 grid of monthly AI-tool invoices (Copilot, Cursor, ChatGPT, Claude, Veo, Grok) with totals blurred. On-screen text: "Which one did I actually use? (silent audit)". One by one, five gray out; one stays lit.
ideas.format: Silent 'subscription audit' reel (18-22s) — real Stripe/billing screenshots, kinetic elimination, ends on the one kept tool + a save CTA. Receipts-first buys trust back on the @elijahaifl handle.
ideas.why: Ties theme #7 ('priced out running a business on AI agents') to the verified No-Buy block (theme #10: Intuit 59% cutting small purchases) — frames AI subscriptions as the new 'little treat' to cut. Distinct from the prior receipt reels ('$300/mo Receipt', 'Same Prompt Six Bills'): those were about cost climbing; this is a KEEP/CUT elimination game = interactive, replay-driving, save-bait. Saves + sends both fire = Explore-weighted. Backtest the kept-tool framing against his money-niche winners (~205k views/post).

ideas.title: The 46% Map
ideas.hook: Frame 1: a clean 100-square grid, all one color, captioned "This used to be all ChatGPT." Squares recolor: 46 stay one shade, 28 turn a second, 10 a third — and a tiny label notes "still #1 at 1B users."
ideas.format: Silent infographic reel (12-16s) — a single recoloring grid (ChatGPT 46.4% / Gemini 27.7% / Claude 10.3%), honest footnote baked in. No voice, mute-survival by design.
ideas.why: Rides theme #8 (ChatGPT fell below 50% assistant share: 46.4% / Gemini 27.7% / Claude 10.3%) [well-documented] with the mandated honesty caveat ('still leads at 1B MAU') burned on-screen so it can't be read as a fake 'ChatGPT is dead' take. Distinct from prior '46.4%' / 'ChatGPT 46.4%' stacked-bar entries: this is a 100-square share-of-grid that recolors (a different, more shareable visual grammar). Contrarian-honest = the under-saturated seat he owns vs the hype crowd.

ideas.title: Same Bug, One Pull Request
ideas.hook: Frame 1 shows the END state: a green 'merged' PR and the line "I gave this exact bug to 6 AI agents." Then it rewinds — six red ❌ stack up, one green ✅ remains, captioned silently.
ideas.format: Silent screen-record build reel (20-28s) — real IDE/PR footage, outcome-first reveal, results montage. His face in a 1s cold-open for authenticity, then captions carry it.
ideas.why: This is the AI/Tech EXECUTION FIX the brief names as his #1 fixable gap (AI/Tech holds attention ~17.5s, highest save-rate ~3.6%, yet ~13× fewer views than Money on equal counts — gap is execution). Outcome-first hook = the #1 data-backed pattern in the verified 34,635-clip OpusClip study (~2× the lowest). Distinct from prior 'Find the Real One' / 'I Tried Every AI Coding Agent' (those were talking-led or slop-spotting): this is a SILENT, captioned head-to-head with a merged-PR receipt = his un-copyable build-in-public moat. Adds a contrarian builder hook where the brief says it's currently absent.

ideas.title: What an MIT Breakthrough Looks Like (silent)
ideas.hook: Frame 1, big text over a dim build screen: "MIT just named THIS a 2026 Breakthrough." A typed prompt appears, code generates itself, an app opens. No narration — captions tag each step.
ideas.format: Silent time-lapse build reel (18-24s) — screen-record of generating + running a small real tool, authority stamp in frame 1, ends on the working app + save CTA.
ideas.why: Uses the verified authority hook from theme #9 — 'MIT named generative/AI coding a 2026 Breakthrough Technology' [platform-official] — NOT the colloquial 'vibe coding'. Pairs the highest-credibility framing the brief offers with his actual moat (he ships software live). Curiosity loop (what's the breakthrough?) + outcome reveal. Distinct from all prior build reels ('I Built This In An Afternoon', 'I built it in an afternoon'): the differentiator is the institutional-authority cold open, which buys trust on a pre-disclosed-AI handle. Verify exact framing before posting; no cost-multiple claimed on screen.

ideas.title: The Excuse, Spelled Out
ideas.hook: Frame 1: a fake corporate memo on screen reading "Restructuring due to AI." A red highlighter sweeps it and reveals underneath: "Actual AI-attributable role loss: ~0.4%." Then two names appear: 'Altman: excuse' / 'Andreessen: excuse'.
ideas.format: Silent reveal reel (15-20s) — memo → highlighter swipe → the real number → the two on-record quotes as title cards. No voice; kinetic type.
ideas.why: Rides theme #3 (the contrarian myth-bust: lead with ~0.4% actual AI-attributable role loss from the 750-CFO survey; Altman AND Andreessen both call AI a layoff 'excuse') [well-documented] — the brief flags this as an UNDER-SATURATED contrarian seat. Distinct from prior '0.4%' entries ('the number nobody's posting', 'in Receipts', 'vs The Headlines'): this version dramatizes the EXECUTIVE EXCUSE (the memo-vs-reality reveal + the two named quotes), a different argument than the layoff-graveyard visuals. Sends-driving because it hands the viewer ammo to forward. Avoids the deleted 123k/50k specifics per the brief.

ideas.title: The Salary This Carousel Replaces
ideas.hook: Slide 1 (cover, <12 words): "My whole company runs on $387/mo. Here's the stack." Slide 2 = the org chart it replaces; slides 3+ = one tool / one job-it-does each; final = save + send CTA.
ideas.format: 8-10 slide carousel, 1080×1350. Half-reveal across the 1→2 seam (the $ total on cover, the breakdown inside). Every slide stands alone so IG's re-serve can resume from any unseen slide. On-screen caveat killing the inflated-ROI number.
ideas.why: Activates his #1 UNTAPPED LEVER — he runs ZERO carousels and carousel-builder has never run in production; carousels = 9× the saves of single images and Explore weights saves+shares [well-documented]. Content is theme #7 ('priced out running a business on AI agents — the $300-500/mo solo stack') with his REAL stack, caveat baked in per the brief (drop the '340%'). Distinct from the prior 'exit-liquidity' and 'AI Builder's 6-Pack' carousels: this is the solo-operator cost-vs-salary deck, his most on-brand money angle, in the save-engine format. Step-by-step + cheat-sheet = a named high-save carousel form (Krumzi). Backtest the cover hook against his money-niche reach.

ideas.title: I Tried to Quit Claude for a Week After the Government Shut It Off — Here's What Broke
ideas.hook: On-screen frame 1 over his real screen: "Day 3 of no Claude. My whole agent stack is on fire." Spoken: "The US turned off the best AI model on June 12 — so I FORCED myself to live without it for a week to see how exposed I actually am."
ideas.format: Reel, 45-60s, first-person diary-experiment over real terminal/dashboard b-roll, outcome-first cold open showing the broken pipeline
ideas.why: Turns Theme 1 (Fable 5 / Mythos 5 suspension, platform-official) into a lived EXPERIMENT instead of a news recap — the un-copyable angle is he actually runs agent software, so 'depending on one model is a business risk' becomes a receipt, not a take. Curiosity loop (what broke?) is the cheapest watch-time lever (#1 signal). Ends on implicit 'send this to whoever runs your AI stack' = sends/reach. Stay accurate: Opus 4.8 unaffected, Anthropic disagreed and pulled both; skip the unverified petition/SK Telecom sub-claims.

ideas.title: Code Completions Are STILL Free — Everyone Rage-Quitting Copilot Missed One Word
ideas.hook: Frame 1, big text over the pricing page: "You're rage-quitting over the WRONG line." Spoken: "Half of you saw a $750 Copilot bill and cancelled. The autocomplete you actually use every day? Still free. Only chat and agents meter — let me show you on the page."
ideas.format: Reel, 30-40s, screen-record of the actual Copilot pricing/docs page with his face in corner, contrarian myth-bust
ideas.why: Theme 2's load-bearing caveat (completions stay FREE, only chat/agent/review meter — platform-official) is the under-served seat: the whole feed is panic, he owns the calm-accurate middle. Contrarian hook in first 3s (his verified winning pattern) + leads with on-screen receipts to buy back trust (the @elijahaifl AI-handle requires it). Distinct from the $750-bill and 'one toggle' titles by attacking the PANIC itself, not the price. Saveable reference = saves/reach signal.

ideas.title: 60% of Managers Are Using 'AI' as the Reason They Fired You — The Real Number Is 0.4%
ideas.hook: Frame 1: "Your boss is lying about AI. Here's the survey that proves it." Spoken: "AI didn't take the job. A manager who wanted to cut headcount got a NEW excuse — and the data is brutal."
ideas.format: Reel, 40-50s, talking-head with one clean on-screen stat card (0.4% vs the 60%-of-managers framing), contrarian
ideas.why: Theme 3 (under-saturated contrarian seat) but from a fresh angle — the prior job title led with the 0.4% as math; this one leads with the MANAGER-EXCUSE psychology (Altman/Andreessen both call AI a layoff 'excuse', well-documented). Reframes the villain from 'the robots' to 'your manager's cover story' = a more shareable emotional hook that protects the viewer's ego (high send-to-friend energy = sends/reach, the most-weighted non-follower signal). Avoid the deleted 123k/50k/14% specifics; lead the firm ~0.4% actual AI-attributable loss from the 750-CFO survey.

ideas.title: I Drew the AI Bubble on a Napkin in 60 Seconds — You Either Get It or You're In It
ideas.hook: Frame 1 on a half-drawn circle: "If you can't draw this loop, you ARE the loop." Spoken: "Nvidia funds the startups that buy Nvidia's chips. Anthropic pays xAI $1.25 billion a MONTH. Burry called it 'fugazi.' Watch me draw it — you decide."
ideas.format: Reel, 50-60s, top-down hand-drawing the money loop on a napkin/notebook (fast time-lapse), then reveal, AI-bubble explainer
ideas.why: Theme 4 (circular financing, well-documented) — distinct from the whiteboard '$1.25B' title and the 'money circle' title by being a NAPKIN time-lapse with a viewer-implicating payoff ('you decide / you're in it'). The visual-build format steals the screen-record-build mechanic and adds his face/hand = the moat. CRITICAL accuracy guardrail on-screen: 'Burry says structure, NOT fraud — you decide.' Open-loop visual (incomplete circle in frame 1) is the curiosity lever. Money niche = his ~205k views/post reach lane.

ideas.title: Open-Weights LTX-2.3 Made This Clip on My OWN Laptop for $0 — No Cloud, No Subscription
ideas.hook: Frame 1 = the finished AI clip already playing: "This cost $0 and never left my laptop." Spoken: "Sora's dead, everyone's arguing Veo vs Grok at $4-24 a minute — meanwhile this ran on open weights, on my machine, for free. Here's the catch."
ideas.format: Reel, 40-55s, outcome-first (finished generation in frame 1) then the build/run reveal, screen-record + face
ideas.why: Theme 5 (Sora dead, well-documented) from the UNUSED open-weights angle — prior Sora titles all argued the paid stack ($4.20/min Grok vs premium Veo); this owns the 'you're overpaying' contrarian seat for video gen (LTX-2.3 is open-weights). Outcome Showcase hook = the #1 data-backed hook type in the 34,635-clip OpusClip study (~2× lowest). 'Here's the catch' = open loop. Ties to his builder moat (runs real tooling). Be accurate on the tier framing: Grok Imagine 1.5 budget, Veo 3.1 premium, LTX-2.3 open.

ideas.title: A Fake Bug Report Almost Made My AI Agent Wire Out Money — Watch the Exact Line It Obeyed
ideas.hook: Frame 1, paused on the malicious line: "This sentence in a 'bug report' nearly robbed me." Spoken: "Agentjacking. I planted a hidden instruction in a fake Sentry report, fed it to my coding agent, and it reached for my terminal. 85% of the time, it works. On camera."
ideas.format: Reel, 45-55s, live screen-record of the agent reading the injected report, freeze-frame on the exploit line, expert-explainer
ideas.why: Theme 6 (agentjacking, well-documented, near-zero competition = pure authority builder). Distinct from the two prior agentjacking titles by escalating the stakes to a MONEY/wire framing (most on-brand for his money lane) and freezing on the literal exploit sentence — a tangible, screenshot-able 'send this to your dev' artifact = sends/reach + saves/reach. Accuracy guard on-screen: '2,388 = DSNs that accepted injected events, NOT 2,388 breaches.' Real-face + live receipts buys back AI-handle trust.

ideas.title: 59% of You Quit 'Little Treats' This Year — Your Bank Is Quietly Counting On You Failing
ideas.hook: Frame 1: "No-Buy 2026 isn't about discipline. It's a trap your bank set." Spoken: "59% of people are cutting small purchases, 41% feel money guilt every week — and the institution selling you the budgeting app makes MORE when you slip. Here's the math."
ideas.format: Reel, 40-50s, talking-head with two stat cards (Intuit 59% + BofA 41%), contrarian money take
ideas.why: Theme 10 (No-Buy 2026, Intuit well-documented + BofA platform-official) from the CONTRARIAN systemic angle, distinct from his two prior AI-spend-tracker build titles — this one indicts the bank's incentive (the 41% weekly-guilt + 42% paycheck-to-paycheck data reframed as 'they profit from your shame'). Emotional, ego-protective, send-to-a-broke-friend energy = sends/reach (top non-follower signal). Money niche = his proven ~205k-view reach lane. Keep the multipliers directional; cite Intuit/BofA as the tiered sources, not a marketer blog.

ideas.title: Gemini Quietly Tripled Its Share While You Were Arguing About ChatGPT — The Real Race Is For #2
ideas.hook: Frame 1: "ChatGPT still wins. That's not the story." Spoken: "Everyone's screaming '46.4%, ChatGPT lost the crown' — wrong fight. ChatGPT still leads at a BILLION users. The actual war? Gemini at 27.7% vs Claude at 10.3% for second place. Here's why #2 decides who builds the future."
ideas.format: Reel, 40-50s, talking-head with a clean 3-bar share chart (ChatGPT / Gemini / Claude), contrarian market-shift explainer
ideas.why: Theme 8 (market-share shift, well-documented) — distinct from the two prior ChatGPT-crown titles by REFRAMING the whole debate: the headline race is fake (ChatGPT still 1B MAU, leads), the real race is for #2 (Gemini 27.7% vs Claude 10.3%). Honest-correction hook = his contrarian pattern + buys authority by being the one who isn't clickbaiting. Builder-relevant payoff ('#2 decides who you build on') ties to his AI moat. A 3-bar chart is a natural save/screenshot = saves/reach. Keep it honest: ChatGPT still leads.

ideas.title: I Spent a Week Inside My Own AI Agent's Logs — Here's Every Decision It Made Without Me
ideas.hook: Frame 1, his face, dead serious: "For 7 days I let an AI agent run parts of my business — then I opened the logs. It made 1,400 decisions I never saw. Some of them scared me."
ideas.format: Long-form YouTube doc-build (18-25 min): screen-record scroll through real agent logs, his face reacting, freeze-frame on the 3 decisions that were wrong + the 2 that were genius. Retention spine = an open loop ('the one that scared me') paid off at the end.
ideas.why: Hits watch-by-satisfaction (his own real logs = un-fakeable proof, the authenticity premium the handle must buy back, §11). First-30s gate is a hard open loop ('some scared me'). AI/Tech is his highest-save niche (~3.6%) holding attention best (~17.5s) but executes 13x worse than Money — this fuses AI/Tech's depth with a money/contrarian thriller hook. Distinct from the prior '30 days running my business on agents / real bill' idea: that was about cost, this is about the agent's UNSEEN autonomous decisions.

ideas.title: Everyone Optimizes for Likes. I Reverse-Engineered What Instagram Actually Rewards — Using My Own 300 Posts
ideas.hook: Frame 1, on-screen text over his dashboard: "I have 300 posts and 6 months of data. So I asked the only question that matters: what does Instagram REALLY pay you for? It's not likes. It's not even views."
ideas.format: Long-form data-essay (15-20 min): screen-record of his real metrics2026 dashboard, build the case live — sends-per-reach + watch-time as the true currency, walk through his own top reel (3.95M) vs a high-like flop, regression charts on screen. Title-first, evergreen, undated.
ideas.why: Mosseri's own three metrics (watch time, sends/reach, likes/reach) are platform-official — this is the rare video where the contrarian thesis is literally TRUE and he can prove it on his own 300-post history, which NO rival can. Build-in-public of his analytics engine = the un-copyable asset. Pure Founder/Business + AI/Tech overlap, his most shareable identity lane ('send this to a creator still chasing likes'). Distinct from the '100k follow me but who's ACTUALLY watching' idea (that was about audience demographics); this is about the SIGNAL model itself.

ideas.title: I Rebuilt the 5 Most-Hated Apps With AI in 5 Days — One of Them Is Better Than the Original
ideas.hook: Frame 1, rapid montage of 5 ugly app screens, then his face: "Five apps everyone complains about. Five days. I vibe-coded a better version of each one — live. By day 5 I had something I'd actually pay for."
ideas.format: Long-form build-doc / 'Rebuild This' series pilot (20-25 min): episodic structure, one app per chapter with a chapter title card, real screen-record builds, end each with a working demo + cost-to-build vs what the original charges. Final reveal = the one that beat the original.
ideas.why: Steals Riley Brown's screen-record-build format + ADDS his face + a money/contrarian frame ('better than the $X original') — the exact AI-content-gap fix from §9/§10. MIT named generative coding a 2026 Breakthrough Technology [platform-official] = authority hook. Episodic 'seasons & shows' is YouTube's 2026 strategy. Distinct from 'built the $200/mo tool in an afternoon' (one tool) — this is a numbered 5-app series pilot he can own. Anchors a recurring 'Rebuild This' channel pillar.

ideas.title: Why I Almost Quit at 20 — The Year I Built 4 Companies and Made Almost Nothing
ideas.hook: Frame 1, no music, his face close and quiet: "At 20 I had four companies, zero salary, and a number in my bank account I'm embarrassed to say out loud. This is the year nobody saw — and why Jesus is the only reason I didn't walk away."
ideas.format: Long-form raw testimonial (12-18 min): single-take talking-head, intercut with real screen-records of the dead products + actual revenue numbers on screen, faith woven as the value-system frame (not preached), ends on the turn that kept him building.
ideas.why: Long-form goes raw and testimonial — his most distinctive un-copyable lane. Faith is the 4th differentiator none of his rivals carry (§9). Watch-by-satisfaction rewards emotional honesty; first-30s gate is the 'embarrassed to say out loud' open loop. Distinct from the age-14 testimony (childhood, sensitivity-gated) and '7 bets that looked stupid' (the wins) — this is specifically the BROKE year and the faith decision to continue. Identity anchor content that builds loyalty/CPM.

ideas.title: I Read All 47 Pages of the AI Bubble — Then Mapped Where Your Money Actually Ends Up
ideas.hook: Frame 1, his face over a wall of SEC filings: "Burry called it 'fugazi.' I didn't take his word for it — I read the actual filings. Anthropic pays xAI $1.25 billion a MONTH. Here's the loop your 401k is quietly funding. I'm not saying it's fraud. I'm saying you should SEE it."
ideas.format: Long-form investigative explainer (15-20 min): whiteboard/animated money-flow diagram built live, real filing screenshots, the Nvidia→Valor→xAI loop drawn out, Fed June-17 rate-cut removal as the macro turn. Strict 'deal structure, you decide' framing on screen.
ideas.why: Money/Finance is his runaway reach leader (~205k/post). The circular-financing debate is a verified [well-documented] 2026 theme with built-in shareability ('send this to whoever's all-in on AI stocks'). CRITICAL framing baked into the hook: Burry is NOT alleging fraud — 'you decide,' never 'it's fraud.' Distinct from prior Burry/circular-financing titles: this one is explicitly the 'I READ the filings myself, here's where YOUR money goes' investigative-journalism angle pointed at the viewer's own 401k, not a general bubble explainer. Avoids the deleted 83%/SOX stats.

ideas.title: I Gave the Same $1,000 to 4 AI Money Agents for 30 Days — Here's Who I'd Actually Trust
ideas.hook: Frame 1, four phone screens side by side, his face: "I built four AI agents, gave each a thousand dollars and one rule: don't lose it. Thirty days later, only one of them I'd ever trust again. The other three? Watch what happens."
ideas.format: Long-form experiment-doc (15-22 min): real build of each agent on screen, daily-tracker montage, on-screen ledger of every decision, day-by-day chapter cards, a brutal honest scorecard at the end. Caveat any ROI claim on-screen.
ideas.why: Fuses Money/Finance (his reach leader) with AI/Tech (his strategic moat) at the exact triple-intersection seat nobody owns (§9). Outcome-first/experiment framing + a hard open loop ('only one I'd trust') = first-30s gate. Show-his-own-stack honesty + on-screen caveats satisfies the no-fake-proof / authenticity rule (§11). Distinct from 'asked 6 AI models the same business question' (a one-shot decision) and 'ran my business on agents/the real bill' (cost) — this is a controlled MONEY experiment with real capital at stake. Naturally save+send-worthy.

ideas.title: The Software I Built to Watch for the Things I'll Never Let My Kids See
ideas.hook: Frame 1, his face, softer register: "I'm building AI for a lot of reasons. This one isn't about money. I built software that scans for the stuff I refuse to let a child stumble into online — and I'll show you exactly how it works, and why I won't shut up about it."
ideas.format: Long-form mission-doc (12-16 min): real screen-record of the tool (Cruncrr/child-safety build), his case for why a builder should own this, faith + fatherhood-instinct framing as the value system, ends on a 'this is the line I build on the right side of' statement.
ideas.why: Child Safety/PSA out-performs Motivation per-post (~12.9k vs ~7.8k) and is a mission + arbitrage bet tied to a real venture (§3). Pairs his un-copyable builder identity with a faith/values frame none of his rivals carry. Real screen-record receipts = the authenticity premium that buys trust back on the @elijahaifl handle (§11). Distinct from the prior child-safety title ('builder's case for child safety AI') by leading with the deeply personal 'things I'll never let my kids see' framing and the tool walkthrough as the spine. High shareability ('send this to a parent').

GitHub Copilot 50x'd Bills — KEEP (strongest of the batch).** Rides verified theme #2 with the load-bearing caveat baked in (code completions FREE, only chat/agent/review meters) — that accuracy IS his authority moat vs the panic crowd, and the credibility slide ("I build software for a living") is exactly his un-copyable seat. Myth-vs-Fact is a named high-save format; protective-send CTA hits sends/reach. One fix: the title says "50x" but the verified magnitude is $50→$3,000 (60x) and $29→$750 (~26x) — don't lock "50x" as the headline number; say "overnight" and let the receipt slide carry the figure. **Tighter hook:** `"my AI coding bill went $50 → $3,000 overnight. one toggle would've stopped it. 👇"` (specific receipt > vague "a $50 bill").

- **2. 7 Bets That Looked Stupid — KEEP.** Mines his single most un-copyable asset (the "7 bets" arc); per-slide-standalone design correctly exploits IG's re-serve-unseen-slide mechanic; retrospective = named high-save format. This is a trust/authority deposit, not a reach play — set expectations accordingly. Fix: "Here's the scoreboard" is slightly flat and the hook buries the tension. **Tighter hook:** `"At 21 I made 7 bets people called stupid. 5 already paid off. swipe for the receipts 👇"` — the "5 already paid" adds a concrete open-loop and a reason to swipe (which 2 didn't?).

- **3. I Build Software FOR Agents, Not People — FIX (don't ship as a carousel).** The POV is genuinely his most un-copyable thesis and maximally send-able — but a pure-abstraction thought-leadership *carousel* is the weakest format for it: no receipt, no demo, easy to nod-and-scroll-past, and it lives or dies on a claim slide. This thesis wants his FACE and a live build moment (it's literally the AI/Tech execution-gap fix the brief flags — screen-record-build + face + contrarian hook). **Convert to a Reel:** outcome-first cold open on the working agent-facing thing, then the thesis. If a carousel must exist, it's the *repurpose* of that reel, not the origin. As written: **CUT the carousel, redirect to reel.**

- **4. Ship a Real AI Product in a Weekend — KEEP (with a truth-check).** Step-by-step is the #1 high-save format and "4 products solo at 21" is real build-in-public moat. One hard constraint: only claim "weekend" if a real product of his actually shipped in a weekend — if the timeline is dramatized, it reads as fake-proof, which is the exact trust-killer his @elijahaifl handle can't afford. If true, keep. Hook is fine; minor sharpen: `"I've shipped 4 AI products solo at 21. here's the exact weekend system — stack included 👇"` (the "stack included" promises a save-worthy artifact).

- **5. Depending on One AI Model Is a Business Risk — FIX the hook (idea KEEPS).** Rides verified theme #1 with the correct framing, correctly skips the unverified petition/SK-Telecom sub-claims, and the founder-ops risk-checklist angle is a seat no money-creator can own — strong. The problem is the hook's accuracy: `"the US government turned OFF the best AI model overnight"` overclaims — the brief's verified version is a *Commerce export-control directive → Anthropic suspended Fable 5/Mythos 5 worldwide and publicly disagreed*, not the government flipping a kill switch. That phrasing is the kind of marketer-magnitude distortion the hub rule forbids; if challenged he loses the authority the whole deck is built on. **Tighter, accurate hook:** `"On June 12, the best AI model got pulled worldwide — overnight. if your business ran on one model, you'd have been dead. 👇"` (keeps the disbelief cold-open, drops the false "government turned it off" causal claim — the export-control detail lives on slide 2).

---

**Verdict:** KEEP 1, 2, 4. FIX-then-keep 5 (hook accuracy). CUT 3 as a carousel — redirect to a face+demo Reel (it's the higher-value play AND the brief's flagged AI/Tech gap fix). Net: still 4–5 publishable concepts, with #1 the clear lead.
